programs.name: ARKansas Medicaid
programs.what_it_does: Provides medical coverage for eligible low-income seniors and disabled individuals, including long-term care services. In Arkansas, it covers nursing home care and coordinates with waiver programs for home-based alternatives.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ and disabled adults meeting income and asset limits.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers doctor visits, hospital stays, prescriptions, and long-term care costs up to state maximums.

programs.name: ARChoices in Homecare
programs.what_it_does: The ARChoices in Homecare program provides home- and community-based care services as an alternative to institutionalization for eligible individuals. Services include Attendant Care, Home-Delivered Meals, Personal Emergency Response Systems, Adult Day Services, Respite Care, and Environmental Adaptations.
programs.who_its_for: Individuals aged 21 and over; those 21-64 must have a physical disability per SSA/SSI, Railroad Retirement, or DHS criteria.
programs.whats_unique: Arkansas-specific Medicaid waiver program offering a tailored range of home-based services beyond standard federal Medicaid, focused on preventing nursing home placement.
programs.benefit_value: Full range of listed services plus standard Medicaid benefits; value varies by individual care plan (e.g., hours of attendant care, meals delivered).

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health and home care, as an alternative to institutional care. In Arkansas, it coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid benefits for participants.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 55+ certified as nursing facility eligible, living in PACE service areas.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage of health and long-term care needs without copays.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Assists with Medicare premiums, deductibles, and cost-sharing through Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), and Qualifying Individual (QI) programs. Arkansas administers these to reduce out-of-pocket costs for eligible seniors.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries aged 65+ meeting specific income/asset thresholds (e.g., QMB up to 100% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: QMB covers all Medicare costs; SLMB/QI cover Part B premiums ($174.70/mo in 2024).

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing groceries to combat food insecurity among seniors. Arkansas issues benefits based on household size and income.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ or households with elderly/disabled members.

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers grants to help pay heating and cooling bills, crisis assistance, and weatherproofing for low-income households. Administered statewide through local agencies in Arkansas.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors and disabled individuals, prioritized for those 60+ or with health vulnerabilities.
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants of $200-1,000 per household annually.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Improves energy efficiency in low-income homes through insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce utility costs. Available via Arkansas agencies serving seniors.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income homeowners/renters aged 60+, with priority for elderly and disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Free services valued at $5,000-8,000 per home.

programs.name: Senior Medicare Patrol (SMP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free Medicare counseling and education to detect/prevent fraud, plus assistance with enrollment and appeals. Operates as SHIP equivalent in Arkansas.[1]
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries 65+, especially those needing billing or coverage help.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious home-delivered meals to homebound seniors, with options for 1-2 meals daily via Area Agencies on Aging. Also offers congregate meals at senior centers.[4][5]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound low-income seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals.
programs.benefit_value: 5-10 meals/week at no/low cost (often free for low-income).

programs.name: Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Supports caregivers of seniors with respite care, training, counseling, and supplemental services to prevent burnout. Provided through Arkansas Area Agencies on Aging.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of adults 60+ with Alzheimer's or related disorders.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $1,600/year per caregiver.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time community service jobs with training for low-income seniors to gain work experience and transition to unsubsidized employment. Administered statewide.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income individuals 55+ (minimum wage jobs at 20 hrs/week).

programs.name: Legal Aid for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal assistance for issues like benefits, housing, and abuse via Area Agencies on Aging and legal aid societies. Includes advice and representation.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing civil legal problems.
programs.benefit_value: Free consultations and representation valued at $200+/hr.

programs.name: Long-term Care Ombudsman
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care. Operates statewide through the Department of Human Services.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities in Arkansas.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy services resolving 80%+ of complaints.

programs.name: Family Caregiver Grant Program
programs.what_it_does: This grant program awards funds to procure respite services for caregivers of seniors with chronic illnesses. It is administered by Alzheimer’s Arkansas in partnership with CareLink.
programs.who_its_for: Caregivers of individuals aged 60+ with chronic illness living in Pulaski County (CareLink service area).
programs.whats_unique: Arkansas-specific grant via local organizations like CareLink and Alzheimer’s Arkansas, providing targeted respite funding not part of federal programs.
programs.benefit_value: Grants for respite services; amount not specified but aimed at enabling caregivers to attend to personal needs (eligibility based solely on care recipient's age, condition, and location).
